cards.title: Sell Lemonade Tycoon: Roll the Recipe
cards.theme: A cozy roadside food/drink stand (lemonade -> boba -> ramen) where rare 'mutation' recipes (Golden Lemon, Rainbow Boba) are the jackpot.
cards.core_loop: Run a food/drink stand -> ROLL random recipes + ingredient mutations (Golden Lemon, Rainbow Boba) that multiply sale price -> place auto-serving stations (offline cash) -> reinvest in stations/luck/new themes (Lemonade -> Boba -> Ramen) -> rebirth to scale.
cards.first_30_seconds: You're handed a beat-up lemonade stand and one giant ROLL button. Tap it -> your lemon shimmers GOLD with an x10 popup -> drop it in the stand and watch coins fountain out. A free second roll hits Rainbow. Hooked before any tutorial text.
cards.depth: The mutation multiplier creates an exponential payout curve where luck investment, station count, and rebirth timing form the optimization spine (the exact pattern Build A Ring Farm rode to ~40k). Theme unlocks gate new mutation pools; recipe completion rewards completionists. The roll stays frictionless, so cooking is a flex/collection layer, not a gate.
cards.why_now: Sell Lemons proves the food-stand fantasy at ~99,015 CCU; Build A Ring Farm (grow-sell incremental with a mutation layer) hit 39,724 CCU at just 61 days. The cooking/food-stand lane is OPEN (fragmented, leader ~22-30%). No food-tycoon-with-RNG-roller exists in the data — clean white space.
cards.incumbent: Sell Lemons (~99,015 CCU) — a pure food-stand cash tycoon with no roll/rarity layer.
cards.differentiation: Injects rarity-chasing into a flat cash-tycoon — the dopamine spike Sell Lemons lacks — while keeping the chill stand fantasy. No food-tycoon-with-RNG-roller exists yet.
cards.risk: The exact fusion has ZERO proven incumbent. Both parents (Sell Lemons, Build A Ring Farm) are independently proven, but the combo is untested execution risk: the offline-tycoon and rarity-chase loops must be balanced so neither cannibalizes the other.
cards.retention: Daily luck-streak login, weekly new mutation tiers + limited mythic recipes, new theme worlds, rebirth prestige ladder, recipe-dex completion buffs.
cards.monetization: 2x luck, 2x cash, auto-roll, auto-collect/offline gamepasses; limited premium mutation banners (event Galaxy odds). Core roll always free.
cards.virality: Group luck multiplier for friends; insane-mutation clips are the thumbnail (a giant glowing Galaxy lemon, 'x1,000,000?!'); show-off display shelf.

evidence_games.name: Sell Lemons
evidence_games.note: Pure food-stand tycoon — proves the fantasy at scale; lacks the rarity hook.

evidence_games.name: Build A Ring Farm
evidence_games.note: Grow-sell incremental with the exact mutation-multiplier layer; ~40k @61d proves the hook scales fast past 1k.

cards.title: Roll-a-Dish: Recipe Gacha Kitchen
cards.tagline: It's Slime RNG, but with dishes.
cards.lane: cooking > Incremental Simulator — OPEN, lane leader ~42%
cards.theme: A bottomless gacha kitchen — tap a Mystery Burner, roll dishes from common to mythic across diners, sushi bars, and a space cafe.
cards.core_loop: Tap a Mystery Burner to roll a random dish (common -> mythic, visible luck stat) -> placed dishes auto-cook and earn offline -> spend on better burners (rarity odds), luck potions, new kitchen-worlds (Diner -> Sushi Bar -> Space Cafe) -> rebirth for permanent luck/multipliers -> recipe-dex completion grants income buffs. It is Slime RNG with dishes instead of slimes.
cards.first_30_seconds: A glowing Mystery Burner sits in front of you with a ROLL button. Tap -> a dish materializes with a rarity beam and a luck number -> it starts auto-cooking and paying out. 'Roll again?' The second roll hits Epic. Pure slot-machine pull, no minigame in the way.
cards.depth: A clean roll->collect->upgrade->unlock loop with a recipe-dex that rewards set completion. Luck odds, burner tiers, and rebirth timing are the optimization spine; kitchen-worlds pace progression. The closest comp (Catch and Cook) gates the roll behind a fishing minigame — removing that friction is the whole bet.
cards.why_now: Catch and Cook! hit ~1,337 CCU at 102 days using exactly this skeleton (RNG-catch + cook + offline + luck + 1.8M combos). Dumpling Squishy Race (Incremental Sim) hit 5,440 CCU in 32 days, proving fresh entrants break 1k fast in this genre. cooking > Incremental Simulator is an open sub-lane.
cards.incumbent: Catch and Cook! (~1,337 CCU, 102d) — fish-only and gates the RNG behind a fishing minigame.
cards.differentiation: Leads with the pure slot-machine roll on ALL food types (no minigame gate), a collectible recipe-dex with set bonuses, and a more generous gacha than any current cooking incremental.
cards.risk: SOFT CEILING. The only direct roll-gacha-on-food comp, Catch and Cook!, tops out at ~1,337 CCU with a mediocre 86.9% rating — the pure-RNG cooking loop clusters at ~1.3k, not 5k+. 1k is achievable but not comfortably exceeded; success leans on a more dopamine-rich gacha and execution.
cards.retention: Daily luck boost, weekly mythic dish drops + limited event recipes, recipe-dex set bonuses, rebirth prestige, kitchen-world unlocks.
cards.monetization: Luck/odds gamepass, auto-roll, 2x cash, offline-time; limited premium recipe banners. Core roll free.
cards.virality: Friends-luck multiplier; rare-dish reveal clips; trade/show-off shelf for mythic dishes.

evidence_games.name: Catch and Cook!
evidence_games.note: Same skeleton (RNG-catch + cook + offline + luck); proves it clears 1k, but caps ~1.3k with an 86.9% rating.

evidence_games.name: Dumpling Squishy Race
evidence_games.note: Incremental Sim; 5.4k in 32 days — fresh entrants break 1k fast in this genre.

cards.title: Baddie Bistro: Collect Chefs, Cook & Rebirth
cards.tagline: Roll rare chefs. Run the empire.
cards.theme: A stylish restaurant empire where the staff are gacha pulls — roll rare 'baddie' chefs and pets to staff your kitchen.
cards.core_loop: Roll randomized chef units (escalating rarity) -> assign to cooking stations (higher rarity = faster/rarer dishes) -> customers auto-pay, income accrues offline -> spend on roll tickets, luck/pets, station upgrades, expansions -> rebirth for permanent luck/cash multipliers -> chef-dex completion for buffs.
cards.first_30_seconds: Your empty diner has one station and a ROLL button. Tap -> a chef pops out with a rarity glow and a speed stat -> drop them on the station, customers stream in, cash ticks up. 'Roll for a rare chef?' The hook is staff-as-loot.
cards.depth: Two proven loops braided: an offline-serve tycoon (build/expand/optimize) and a rarity-chase (roll chefs/pets, complete the dex). Station synergies and chef placement add a light optimization puzzle; rebirth resets pace progression for 100h+.
cards.why_now: Run a Restaurant! (19,363 CCU @75d) and My Gaming Cafe! (4,011 @32d) prove fresh offline-serve tycoons clear 1k fast. The exact hybrid already prototypes as 'My Baddie Restaurant!' at 852 CCU @59d with a standout 0.275 ccu-per-1k-visits (high stickiness) — on the cusp of 1k with a thin build.
cards.incumbent: Run a Restaurant! (19,363 CCU) and Restaurant Tycoon 3 — pure build/serve tycoons with NO gacha.
cards.differentiation: Incumbents are pure build/serve tycoons. This injects the Slime-RNG collection spine (roll rare chefs, luck, pets, dex). Out-polish and out-content the rough 'My Baddie Restaurant' prototype.
cards.risk: The RNG-chef + tycoon fusion is UNPROVEN at 1k+ — the single direct precedent sits at 852 CCU (below the bar), and every game above 1k here is a pure tycoon with no gacha. The bet hinges on gacha LIFTING the game past entrenched pure-tycoon incumbents rather than just adding monetization friction.
cards.retention: Daily login chef ticket, weekly new chef rarities + limited event chefs, chef-dex set bonuses, rebirth ladder, restaurant theme expansions.
cards.monetization: Roll-ticket gamepass, luck/2x cash, auto-collect, VIP station slot; limited event chef banners. Core loop ungated.
cards.virality: Show off your rarest chef; friends-luck multiplier; 'I pulled a SECRET chef' clip bait; light trading.

evidence_games.name: Run a Restaurant!
evidence_games.note: Fresh offline-serve restaurant tycoon, 19k @75d — proves the tycoon spine clears 1k fast (no gacha).

evidence_games.name: My Baddie Restaurant!
evidence_games.note: The exact RNG-chef + restaurant-tycoon hybrid, 852 @59d with high stickiness (0.275 ccu/1k visits) — on the cusp on a thin build.

cards.title: Hatch a Pet: Roll, Steal & Trade Tycoon
cards.tagline: Hatch rare pets. Steal your friend's.
cards.lane: pet (Pet Care, 431k demand) — CROSS-LANE bet: NOT open, Adopt Me holds 58% (concentrated). Play is importing the steal-meta into pet-hatch RNG.
cards.theme: A cozy pet ranch where you hatch adorable rare pets that earn coins, with a daring steal-and-trade layer that turns rare pets into social currency.
cards.core_loop: Tap an egg to hatch a random pet (common to secret, visible luck/rarity) -> pets passively earn coins on your ranch (offline) -> spend coins on better eggs (rarity odds), luck potions, ranch expansions -> open a timed STEAL window to snatch a rival's displayed pet (or safely TRADE for one) -> chase dex completion for permanent income set-bonuses -> rebirth for luck/coin multipliers and new biome-themed egg worlds. Pure Slime-RNG roll->collect->upgrade->unlock with steal/trade as the social hook.
cards.first_30_seconds: You spawn on a small ranch with one egg glowing on a pedestal and a coin counter. Tap: the egg wobbles, cracks, a cute pet pops out with a rarity burst and a coin trickle starts. A banner: 'A rare pet is on display next door — STEAL or TRADE?' One tap to roll again, one tap to peek next door. Roll-collect-earn lands in under 20 seconds, instantly legible to a young audience.
cards.depth: Depth lives in the economy and social layer: optimizing luck-vs-income reinvestment, dex set-bonus planning (keep vs fodder), and a player-driven trade market where rare pets accrue social value. The steal window adds risk management (display your best pet for flex, or vault it for safety?). Scales via deepening rarity tiers, mutations, and rebirth multipliers — a proven incremental ceiling — while staying legible.
cards.why_now: Honest correction: pet is NOT flagged open — it's a single Pet Care lane (431,563) with Adopt Me at 58% (concentrated, monolith-leaning). The thesis is to import the hot steal-meta (403k) and the proven harvest×steal pattern (Grow a Garden 2, 821k) into a pet-hatch RNG loop — the PUREST fit for the user's Slime-RNG taste reskinned to pets. roll 1.21, lucky 1.30, hide 1.58 support the RNG + steal/stealth layer. Risk: entering against a 58% incumbent, so differentiation must carry it.
cards.incumbent: 'Adopt Me!' (58% of Pet lane) — a pet-CARE/roleplay/trading game, NOT a roll-incremental. 58% means concentrated, not open; beating it head-on is hard. Realistic targets are smaller pet-hatch incrementals (solo coin-grind, no social steal stakes) — beat THEM by fusing the cleanest Slime-RNG loop with a steal/trade social layer and a generous dex-driven gacha.
cards.differentiation: Adopt Me is trading + roleplay with no incremental dopamine; pet-hatch sims are solo coin-grinds with no social stakes. This sits in the gap: a tight roll-collect-upgrade incremental (the loop the user loves) with an optional steal/trade layer that makes rare pets socially meaningful, and a dex with income set-bonuses rewarding completion over pure luck. Differentiation must be sharp because the leader is at 58%.
cards.retention: Daily luck streak, weekly new pet/egg rarity drops and biome worlds, limited event eggs, a live trade economy that self-sustains engagement, rebirth ladder, dex set-bonus chase. Trading + stealing keep friend groups checking each other's ranches.
cards.monetization: Luck gamepass, auto-hatch, extra pet slots, VIP egg, limited event-pet gacha, 2x coins / 2x luck (group-buyable), trade-slot expansion, pet-vault (anti-steal) pass. All accelerators — the dex and trade economy stay skill/social-driven.
cards.virality: Trading and stealing are inherently social and clip-worthy ('traded my secret pet', 'he STOLE my mythic'); group luck multipliers reward squads; rare-hatch reactions are thumbnail gold. The pet aesthetic is broadly appealing to the young Roblox audience.

evidence_games.name: Pet Care lane demand (concentrated, NOT flagged open)
evidence_games.note: Real number from brief, corrected: Pet Care lane with Adopt Me at 58% leader (concentrated). No 'Pet>Incremental sub-lane OPEN' flag exists. Large demand is real; openness is not.

evidence_games.name: Steal-brainrot meta (social steal hook)
evidence_games.note: Real (brief: 'hot meta, OPEN'). Proves the steal/snatch window is a hot, retained social mechanic — the genuine importable differentiator for this cross-lane bet.

evidence_games.name: Grow a Garden 2 (harvest x steal proven)
evidence_games.note: Real (brief: garden 821,241; harvest x steal a proven combo). Proves steal/trade-on-collectibles is a mega-scale retained pattern, de-risking the steal+trade layer.

cards.risk: Adopt Me (250k) and Pet Simulator dominate pet-hatch; differentiation rests entirely on the steal/trade social layer surviving against entrenched giants.

cards.title: Unbox a Tycoon: Crate Factory
cards.tagline: Open crates, build a loot empire, rebirth
cards.lane: unboxing (Tycoon) — OPEN, most fragmented leader of any lane (23%), 21,261 de-contaminated demand
cards.theme: You run a crate-opening factory. Conveyor belts feed mystery crates into your unboxer; what pops out (pets, gems, relics across a rarity ladder) lands on display pedestals that generate passive income. Richer collection wall -> faster belts -> bigger crates. Industrial-arcade loot factory you physically grow on a plot.
cards.core_loop: Open crate (RNG reveal) -> collected item auto-displays and generates idle cash by rarity -> cash upgrades belt speed, crate tier, and luck -> unlock new factory wings (Pets -> Gems -> Mythic Beasts -> Cosmic) -> rebirth to a prestige factory for permanent luck+income multiplier. The Slime-RNG collection loop with a buildable Tycoon plot as the display surface.
cards.first_30_seconds: You walk onto an empty factory plot with one crate and a giant ROLL button. First crate is rigged to an 'Epic' pet that flies to a pedestal and immediately starts spitting visible coins. A second locked crate tier glints right next to you. Collect -> idle income -> visible next unlock, all in the first 20 seconds.
cards.depth: Two intersecting economies: the RNG collection (chase rarity, complete sets) and the Tycoon layout (placement matters — adjacency bonuses, themed-wing multipliers, belt-routing optimization). Players optimize a physical factory build AND a collection. Prestige resets add a meta-layer of luck-stat investment.
cards.why_now: unboxing is OPEN with the most fragmented leader of any lane (23% — vs garden 59%, pet 58%, merge 76%) and a real 21,261 demand after stripping 93% mega-title bleed (honest figure). It sits in Tycoon and directly executes the flagged Incremental-Simulator x Tycoon untapped combo (512 vs 451, 0 combined) — the biggest structural arbitrage in the brief. roll 1.21, lucky 1.30, build 1.32, tower 1.49 all over-index in hits.
cards.incumbent: No named in-lane benchmark exists — the unboxing leader is unnamed at 23%, which is both the opportunity and the risk (no proven 1k+ title to study in-lane). The studyable cross-lane benchmark is 'Run a Restaurant!'-style Tycoon retention applied to an unboxing reveal.
cards.differentiation: Most unboxing games are pure case-openers (open, see number, repeat) with thin retention; most tycoons lack the RNG dopamine. This makes the loot you unbox INTO the income engine of a buildable factory — reveal and tycoon are the same object. No combined Incremental-Sim x Tycoon game exists in the corpus, so this is uncontested whitespace.
cards.retention: Daily free crate (streak-scaled luck), weekly limited crate theme, prestige/rebirth ladder, set-completion display bonuses, leaderboard for factory income/hour, friends-server group-luck multiplier. New factory wing every weekly update feeds the unlock treadmill.
cards.monetization: 2x Luck pass, Auto-Open pass (idle crate opening), 2x Income pass, limited weekly premium crates (gacha), rebirth-skip token, VIP pedestal slots, cosmetic factory skins. No fun gated — luck passes change odds-to-good, not access.
cards.virality: 'Secret pet' reveals are inherently shareable; a wall of rare loot is a screenshot flex. Group-luck draws friends. Thumbnail of a rainbow crate exploding into a giant mythic with 'I GOT THE 1-IN-A-MILLION' is proven high-CTR. Trading (later) adds social stickiness.

evidence_games.name: Unboxing-lane leader (23%, unnamed)
evidence_games.note: DERIVED, not measured: ~23% of the 21,261 de-contaminated demand. Most fragmented leader of any OPEN lane = low bar to top, but no named comp to study in-lane.

evidence_games.name: Merge a Nuke!
evidence_games.note: DERIVED (~76% of 35,249 merge demand). Caveat: this is the MONOLITH the brief says AVOID; cited only as weak proof a single-mechanic Tycoon reveal can hold 5-figure CCU, not as a model to copy.

evidence_games.name: Incremental-Sim x Tycoon combo (untapped)
evidence_games.note: Brief's flagged #1 arbitrage: 512 vs 451 games, 0 combined. Structural whitespace this card executes — novelty is the upside and the unproven-retention risk.

cards.risk: Unboxing's REAL demand is thin once the keyboard mega-title bleed is removed (~21k); the lane is open but small — 1k is reachable, but big upside is less certain.

cards.title: Hatch & Hold: Monster Tower Survival
cards.tagline: Hatch the army. Hold the night.
cards.lane: survive x hatch (untapped combo) / tower-defense survival
cards.theme: You defend a glowing crystal at the center of an island; waves of monsters attack each night, and you hatch your OWN monsters from eggs to fight for you.
cards.core_loop: Open eggs to hatch random monster defenders (common to mythic, visible power + luck stat) -> place them around your crystal as living towers -> survive escalating night waves that drop currency and rarer eggs -> spend on better eggs (rarity odds), hatch-luck, and unit upgrades/merges -> push wave milestones that unlock new biomes and egg tiers -> rebirth for permanent luck/damage multipliers and a collection dex. Roll -> place -> survive -> upgrade -> roll better.
cards.first_30_seconds: You land next to a glowing crystal with a single egg pulsing in front of you and a countdown: 'NIGHT 1 IN 0:20.' You tap the egg, it cracks, a cute monster pops out and auto-walks to a tower slot. The countdown hits zero, a small wave shuffles in, your monster zaps them, coins fountain out, and a 'NEW EGG UNLOCKED' banner pops. Roll-place-defend delivered before the first minute ends.
cards.depth: Tower-defense placement + unit synergy gives a real strategy layer (elemental counters, AoE vs single-target, buff/aura monsters, choke-point mazing) on top of the RNG collection chase. Co-op adds role specialization (tank/buff/nuke). The push-vs-bank tension (spend coins on this run's defense or save for a rarer egg?) and the merge/fodder economy give a deep, self-teaching loop that scales to 100+ hours via endless wave/biome escalation.
cards.why_now: survive x hatch is an explicit UNTAPPED combo — both parent tags popular yet ~never shipped together, so it's genuine white space. Both parents over-index among 1k+ winners: survive 1.21, stacked with tower 1.49, defense 1.53, monster 1.36, roll 1.21, lucky 1.30 — the most hit-correlated mechanic cluster in the dataset, all feeding one loop. Rides the proven hatch-RNG dopamine the user loves. Caveat: the fusion itself has zero shipped precedent — novelty is upside and unproven risk.
cards.incumbent: Closest analogs are pure tower-defense games (the Skibi/Toilet Tower Defense family — general knowledge, not in the brief) and pure pet-hatch sims. No single game owns 'hatch your own monsters AND survive tower-defense waves.' Beat TD incumbents by adding the addictive hatch-collection spine; beat hatch-sims by adding real survival stakes and co-op.
cards.differentiation: Tower-defense games hand you fixed shop units; this makes your roster an RNG-hatched, mergeable, rarity-chase collection — the Slime-RNG loop powering a TD game. Hatch-sims have no stakes; here your monsters can FAIL the wave, making each roll consequential. Co-op survival makes it social where most hatch-sims are solo grinds.
cards.retention: Endless escalating waves + biome unlocks give an infinite progression ladder, weekly new egg/monster drops and limited mythic events, daily luck streak, prestige/rebirth with permanent multipliers, a monster dex with set auras. Co-op nights pull friends back nightly.
cards.monetization: Luck/hatch-speed gamepass, auto-hatch, extra unit slots, VIP egg, limited event eggs (gacha), 2x coins and 2x luck (group-buyable), revive-the-crystal token. Accelerators only — placement and merging skill still matter, so spend never trivializes the fun.
cards.virality: Co-op 'we survived to night 100' clips, rare-hatch reactions, and squad luck multipliers drive play-with-friends spread. Monster designs are inherently meme/thumbnail-able. The wave-milestone leaderboard fuels competitive sharing.

evidence_games.name: survive x hatch combo (untapped, per brief)
evidence_games.note: Listed in brief UNTAPPED COMBOS: both parent tags popular, ~never combined, zero shipped fusion. Novelty is upside AND unproven retention risk. No 1k+ proof exists for the fusion.

evidence_games.name: Tower/defense tag lift among 1k+ winners
evidence_games.note: Brief gives LIFT not CCU: tower 1.49, defense 1.53 over-index in 1k+ winners — TD is a proven high-retention skeleton that de-risks the survival half.

evidence_games.name: Roll/lucky tag lift among 1k+ winners
evidence_games.note: Brief gives LIFT not CCU: roll 1.21, lucky 1.30 over-index in winners — proves hatch-RNG collection dopamine scales. Only the fusion of the two halves is unproven.

cards.risk: survive x hatch is an untapped combo with NO positive proof of the fusion at 1k+; tower-defense wave balancing is heavier to build than a pure idle loop.
